visualGoal: Make a buyer measure their room before comparing data sheets: photometric for shallow rooms, radar/fusion for depth or outdoor range, overhead for tall ceilings. Real launch-monitor product proof for the radar/photometric/fusion contrast; no procedural art.

realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: Image file is on disk but NOT in verified-product-images.json, so it is text-only (link + Amazon) in the overhead section per the image rule. Vision-verify and add to the verified list before it can become a gearpick/shotgrid tile.
Image file on disk but NOT in verified-product-images.json; text-only in the overhead section. Vision-verify and add before promoting to a body visual.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic home golf simulator bay lit warm gold against deep green walls, a photometric camera unit set beside a hitting mat with a radar unit positioned behind the tee to contrast the two sensor positions, open product-overlay space for real launch monitors, a tape measure on the floor to signal 'measure your room first,' no fake branded hardware, no fake UI, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, fake launch monitor, warped screen, impossible geometry, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art

infographicPrompts.title: Where each sensor sits, and the room it needs
content: Radar: behind you, ~15-20 ft depth ideal
Fusion: behind you, more forgiving than pure radar
Photometric: beside the ball, works down to ~12 ft
Overhead: above the ball, needs 9-10 ft ceiling
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center diagram, four sensor positions around a hitting spot, large readable text, golf greens/golds, real LM photos as position markers for Codex overlay

infographicPrompts.title: Match the sensor to your tape measure
content: Depth or outdoor range: radar or fusion
9-10 ft ceiling + premium club data: overhead
Test flicker-free lighting before any camera unit
infographicPrompts.style: Numbered ladder, product-photo placeholders for Codex overlays

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult golf curator Nina Brooks holding a tape measure in a partly-finished basement simulator space, gesturing at the depth behind the hitting spot, confident advisor energy, product overlay space beside her, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait.

openQuestions: Vision-verify and add Uneekor EYE XO2 and Foresight Falcon to verified-product-images.json so the overhead section can show units instead of text-only links?
Add verified Garmin R50 and FlightScope Mevo Gen 2 as gearpicks in a later pass for more visual density, or keep the set focused on the radar/photometric/fusion contrast?
Resolve the room-depth figures to a single authoritative manufacturer spec if a range (18-20 / <15 / 12 ft) is not acceptable for certification?
